key_points: Electric current flowing through a conductor produces a magnetic field around it
The magnetic effect of electric current was discovered by Hans Christian Ørsted
Magnetic field lines show the direction and strength of a magnetic field
A solenoid is a long coil of wire that produces a magnetic field when current passes through it
An electromagnet is a temporary magnet made by passing current through a coil around an iron core
examples: Compass needle deflecting when placed near a current-carrying wire
Crane magnets lifting iron scrap in factories
Electric motor used in fans and mixers
formulas: Magnetic field → Region around a magnet or current-carrying conductor where magnetic force can be felt
Right-hand thumb rule → Used to find the direction of magnetic field around a current-carrying conductor
exam_tips: Remember the discovery of magnetic effect of electric current
Learn the definition of magnetic field and electromagnet
Know simple uses of electromagnets and electric motors
